data.title: BioCryst Pharmaceuticals appoints Jon P. Stonehouse as interim CFO
data.description:
• BioCryst Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:BCRX) said on Thursday that it has appointed Jon P. Stonehouse, the company’s Chief Executive Officer, to serve as interim Chief Financial Officer and interim Principal Accounting Officer, effective as of the effective date. 
  • The company has also hired a former public biotechnology company CFO as a financial consultant to support Stonehouse while the company continues its search for a new CFO. 
  • The company had announced recently that CFO Anthony Doyle will resign from the company, effective April 9.

data.title: Musk says DOGE to save $150B in fiscal 2026, down from earlier $1T view
data.description:
Elon Musk said Thursday that the White House’s Department of Government Efficiency expects to cut around $150B for fiscal 2026, down from his projection of nearly $1T.

"Thanks to your fantastic leadership, the amazing Cabinet, the very talented DOGE team, I'm excited to announce that we anticipate savings in F26 from reduction of waste and fraud by $150 billion," Musk told a Cabinet meeting.

However, Musk recently told Fox News that DOGE would cut around $1T by the end of his time as the head of the department. Musk’s designation as a special government employee is limited to 130 days, and that term is due to end next month. 

President Donald Trump said he hoped to keep other members of DOGE’s team, but many are also classified as special government employees. 

"Your people are fantastic, in fact, hopefully they will stay around for the long haul," he said. 

The White House did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha’s request for comment concerning the lower savings estimate by Musk. 

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Northwest Healthcare Properties Real Estate Investment Trust sells remaining stake in Assura for $139.3M
data.description:
• Northwest Healthcare Properties Real Estate Investment Trust (TSX:NWH.UN:CA) on Thursday announced the sale of its remaining interest in Assura, totalling 163.3M shares, through an on-market transaction for gross proceeds of about $139.3M (£76.8M).
  • The selling price represents a gain of over 20% to the REIT's December 31, 2024, IFRS book value. The proceeds will be used to repay debt and for general trust purposes.
  • The sale results in the REIT completing the disposition of its UK portfolio for total proceeds of around $917.3M (£514.6M), which is a gain of about $32.3M (£14.6M) relative to the price transacted at in August 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: FDA to phase out animal testing requirement, adopt AI methods
data.description:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration said on Thursday that it will be phasing out animal testing requirement for monoclonal antibody therapies and other drugs, replacing it by more ethical methods like AI computations and human-based models.

In a move to change the testing methods to a more moral approach and advance public health, the agency said that the current animal testing requirement will be taken over by approaches, including AI-based computational models of toxicity and cell lines and organoid toxicity testing in a laboratory setting.

"Implementation of the regimen will begin immediately for investigational new drug (IND) applications, where inclusion of NAMs data is encouraged, and is outlined in a roadmap also being released today," FDA mentioned in a statement.

To make determinations of efficacy, the agency said it will also begin using existing safety data from other countries, with comparable regulatory standards, where the drug has already been through human trials.

"By leveraging AI-based computational modeling, human organ model-based lab testing, and real-world human data, we can get safer treatments to patients faster and more reliably, while also reducing R&D costs and drug prices. It is a win-win for public health and ethics,” said FDA Commissioner Martin A. Makary.

The agency, over the coming year, will be launching a pilot program allowing some monoclonal antibody developers to use a primarily non-animal-based testing strategy, under FDA consultation.

Charles River Laboratories (NYSE:CRL) and rival Inotiv (NASDAQ:NOTV) are among some leading contract research organizations focused on animal trials who could be affected by this move.

data.title: Logitech withdraws FY 2026 outlook citing tariff environment
data.description:
• Logitech International (NASDAQ:LOGI) on Thursday reaffirmed its financial outlook for its fiscal year ended March 31, 2025. 
  • However, Logitech withdrew its outlook for fiscal year 2026 citing the continuing uncertainty of the tariff environment. 
  • Logitech confirmed its current FY2025 outlook of between $4.54 billion and $4.57 billion in net sales, representing year-over-year sales growth of 5.4% and 6.4% in US dollars, and 6.2% to 7.1% in constant currency, and non-GAAP operating income between $755 million and $770 million.
  • Logitech International (NASDAQ:LOGI) shares were down 0.57% in extended trading. 
  •

data.title: Palatin Technologies shares fall as it receives NYSE American delisting notice
data.description:
• Palatin Technologies (NYSE:PTN) said on Thursday that the NYSE American publicly announced and provided a notice to the company that it has determined to commence proceedings to delist the company's common stock from NYSE American.
  • NYSE Regulation has determined the company is no longer suitable for listing pursuant as it was unable to demonstrate that it had regained compliance with sections related to stockholders' equity requirements, by the end of the maximum 18-month compliance plan period, which expired on April 10, 2025.
  • The company intends to appeal such determination to delist the common stock. The company expects the common stock to continue to trade on NYSE American during the appeal process.
  • Palatin Technologies (NYSE:PTN) shares fell more than 13% in extended trading.

data.title: Justice Department to maintain corporate monitors for TD Bank - report
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Justice will maintain corporate monitors for TD Bank (NYSE:TD), as the Trump administration evaluates their use on a broader scale, according to a Thursday media report.

Independent third-party monitors, responsible for reviewing compliance policies, are sometimes required for companies as part of a settlement with the Justice Department to resolve accusations of criminal misconduct.

In recent weeks, the DOJ halted monitorships as part of an ongoing review into whether certain monitors should end ahead of schedule.

The review is ongoing, though the DOJ has opted to keep the monitors in place for Canada's TD (NYSE:TD), Reuters reported, citing two people familiar with the matter.

As part of a deal last year, TD (TD) was required to retain an independent monitor after pleading guilty to violating a federal anti-money laundering statute.

data.title: Freeport, Teck, Ivanhoe shares plunge but JPM stays bullish on copper supply challenges
data.description:
Base metal miners including Alcoa (AA), Freeport-McMoRan (NYSE:FCX) and Teck Resources (TECK) tumble Thursday, -9.7%, -8.2% and -6.1% respectively, as rising tensions between the U.S. and China weigh on optimism for global growth prospects and therefore industrial metals demand forecasts.

J.P. Morgan analyst Bill Peterson cuts his price targets for equities in the metals space due to potentially lower commodity pricing "amid ongoing tariff uncertainty and evolving recession risks," and chops PTs for Alcoa (AA) to $28 from $40, Freeport (NYSE:FCX) to $42 from $52; and Teck (TECK) to C$58 from C$72 ahead of the companies' upcoming Q1 earnings results on April 16, April 24 and April 24, respectively.

Peterson anticipates only marginal tariff-related impacts to the companies' Q1 results, with investors likely focused on forward-looking commentary that could prove underwhelming as companies grapple with trade policy moves, but "the stakes have changed," with JPM’s Global Economists seeing a 60% probability of a global recession and JPM's China economists now forecasting a -0.7% drag on GDP plus another wave of fiscal stimulus.

On Neutral-rated Alcoa (AA), Peterson anticipates guidance will be hurt by tariffs on Canadian-based exports to the U.S., and suspect greater initial out-of-pocket funding requirements for its new joint venture at its loss-making San Ciprian complex.

Peterson says his March upgrade of Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) to Overweight was premature, as he underestimated the magnitude of President Trump's reciprocal tariffs and subsequent pullback; however, the positive thesis holds that tariff risk likely will maintain premium pricing for the company's U.S.-based footprint for the foreseeable future, combined with expected long-term supply challenges supporting a favorable through-cycle pricing environment for the copper industry.

The analyst remains Overweight on Teck (TECK), citing balance sheet strength that should support stock buybacks and debt reduction; growth optionality is a plus, but Peterson questions how and if a recession would impact project sanctioning expected later this year.

Peterson also sees Overweight-rated Ivanhoe Electric (IE) as a solid option for longer-term investors, especially ahead of the planned June release of its feasibility study and updated MRE for the flagship Santa Cruz project; more broadly, the potential classification of copper as a critical mineral servews as an "attractive call option" for U.S.-exposed miners Ivanhoe and Freeport (FCX); Ivanhoe -11.8% on Thursday.

data.title: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman leaves door open to Pentagon, but is cautious
data.description:
OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said he wouldn’t completely rule out the possibility of collaborating with the U.S. Department of Defense on advanced weapons systems, though he made clear such a scenario would be unlikely in the near term, Bloomberg News reported on Thursday.

Speaking at the Vanderbilt Summit on Modern Conflict and Emerging Threats on Thursday, Altman acknowledged that unforeseen global developments could shape future decisions.

“I will never say never, because the world could get really weird,” he was quoted as saying. He added that involvement in weapons development would only be considered in highly constrained scenarios where few alternatives exist.

“I don’t think most of the world wants AI making weapons decisions,” Altman added during a discussion with Paul Nakasone, the former National Security Agency director and a current member of OpenAI’s board.

The idea of tech companies supporting military programs has long been divisive, particularly among employees at major firms. Notably, Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) faced internal protests in 2018 over its work on Project Maven, a Pentagon initiative involving AI. However, attitudes within the artificial intelligence industry have started to evolve, with some companies showing increased willingness to engage with defense-related projects.

OpenAI itself has revised its approach over the past year. In December, it partnered with Anduril Industries, a defense technology startup, to develop anti-drone systems — marking a clear step into the national security space.

Altman also stressed the importance of helping the U.S. government become more adept at adopting AI tools. 

The event, attended by members of the intelligence community, military personnel and academic experts, took place just ahead of OpenAI’s anticipated release of its next major reasoning model, GPT-4.5 (also referred to as "03"), which is expected next week, Bloomberg News reported.

data.title: Two American Airlines planes clip wings at Reagan National
data.description:
• The U.S. Federal Aviation Administration has launched an investigation into two American Airlines (NASDAQ:AAL) regional jets that clipped wings while on the runway at Reagan National Airport in Washington.
  • There were no injuries and no impact to flight operations at the airport. Both aircraft have been taken out of service to be inspected for damage.
  • According to sources, a Bombardier (OTCPK:BOMBF) CRJ 900 headed to Charleston, South Carolina and an Embraer (ERJ) E175 headed to NYC, both operated by American (NASDAQ:AAL) clipped wings at around 12:45pm ET. The collision removed the winglet from one of the aircraft.
  • The news had a modest impact on shares, which were already deep in negative territory amid a broad-based market sell-off.
